def: adj. (f. -ī), Lean, thin, meagre; poor, barren; weak, feeble.
more: adj. S. दुर्बल, from dur neg., bal strength. See patlā , 3. 1. Duble kalāṅvat kī kaun suntā hai? Prov. Who listens to a poor singer? (Who ever heeds the voice of the poor?). 2. ' Kyoṅ kyoṅ, re bhaīyā, dublā kyoṅ, aur kyoṅ terā mailā bhes?' 'Bāṭ chalat, jījī, dūblā, dhūp paṛat mailā bhes .' Suhāg. 'Why why, my brother, art so thin, why browned thy face? 'Travelling on foot hath made me thin, sunburnt my face!' dublā-pan, dublāpā , n. f. Leanness; emaciation.
adj. Thin, lean, poor, barren, meagre; weak. دبلاپا يا پنا dublā-pā or -panā, s. m. Leanness.

def: adj. See دبلا dublā.

def: s.f. = dublāpā, q.v. s.m. Leanness, meagreness, thinness, emaciation; weakness.
s. f. Thinness, leanness, meagreness.

ety: ḍūb˚, per met. = būḍ˚ = Prk. बुड्ड(इ) = S. व्रुड(ति), rt. व्रुड्
def: v.n. To dive; to sink, drown, be drowned; to drown oneself; to be immersed, be submerged, inundated, deluged, or flooded; to be lost, be sacrificed (as capital, reputation, &c.); to be destroyed, be ruined; to sink, go down, set (as the sun); to sink, faint (as the heart); to be absorbed, be engrossed, be lost (in business, or study, or thought, &c.)
more: v. n. 1. To dive. 2. To drown. 3. To be immerged or deluged. 4. To set, to sink; to be destroyed or ruined. 5. To be absorbed (in business, study, &c.). دل ڐوبنا dil-ḍūbnā, To faint.
subs: v.n. To be drowned, die by drowning; to drown oneself.

def: v. n. 1. To drown; to be immersed, or deluged. 1. Tairegā so ḍūbegā . Prov. Who swims sinks. 2. Lāo kuāṅ! maiṅ ḍūbūṅ . Prov. Bring the well! I'll drown myself. 3. Kāgaz kī nāo āj na ḍūbī, kal ḍūbī . Prov. A paper boat, if not to-day, to-morrow sure will sink. 4. Ḍūbte ko tinke kā sahārā bahut . Prov. A drowning man will catch at a straw. 5. Woh din ḍubbe jo ghoṛī chaṛheṅ kubbe . Prov. The days are gone when humpbacks rode. (Said to the incompetent.) 6. Woh ḍūbeṅ maṅjhdhār jin par bhārī bojh . Prov. He sinks midstream who bears a heavy load of sins. 7. Āp ḍūbe bāmnā. jijmānoṅ le ḍūbe . Prov. When the Brahman sinks his constituents go down with him. 8. Ḍāl oṛhnā gal meṅ tere, donoṅ mūchh ukhāṛūṅ! Ḍūb marūṅ yā nikal jāūṅgī, ijjat teri bigāṛūṅ . Play. I'll drag you with my sheet around your neck and pluck out your moustache, I'll leave your house and drown myself, but foul disgrace shall you abash. 9. Kahīṅ ḍūbe bhī tire hain? Prov. What has sunk will never swim again. 2. To set; sink. Sūraj ḍūbnā . 3. To be engrossed, absorbed, buried, wrapt in. 4. To be ruined, lost, smashed, done for. Rupayā ḍūb gayā . 5. To be disgraced; to get a bad name. Nām ḍūbnā. ḍūbā-huā kharch . Expenditure without return ( baṭṭe khāte ). ḍūbā-huā khātā , An account or register of bad debts. ḍūb mar'nā , v. n. To drown oneself. ḍūban beṛā , n. m. lit . a sinking ship. An unnatural offence; a damning sin. ḍūban beṛā paṛnā , v. n. To be committed (a damning sin). ḍūbant yā burī ṭīp . A dead or bad bond. ḍūbant khātā , An irrecoverable balance.
v.t. = ḍubonā, q.v. v.t. = ḍubāwnā = ḍubānā, q.v.
v.t. = ḍubāwnā = ḍubānā, q.v.
more: (caus. of ڐوبنا) v. a. To drown, immerse, flood; to ruin, &c. see ڐبانا ḍubānā. ڐبودينا ḍubo denā, v. a. To exhaust, to ruin, to demolish, to drown.

def: v. a. 1. To drown; immerse; submerge; flood. 1. Nāo kinne ḍaboī ? Khuāja khizar ne . Prov. Who drowned the boat? Khwaja Khizar. 2. Bhūl gaë sab gyān shāstar paṛh-kar sabhī ḍaboyā . Play. Chaubolā . In Shastras learned, sense forsook, the house and all in ruin laid. 2. To spoil; destroy; ruin. 3. To give in marriage to a poor or impotent man. nām ḍubonā , v. a. To bring disgrace on one's name.

more: adj. Unknown, insignificant, helpless, hiding through weakness.

ety: v.n. fr. دبر 'to follow at the back'
def: s.f. The back; the hinder part (of a thing); the backside, posteriors, buttocks, the podex; — the latter part (of), the end, conclusion (as of a month; of prayers, &c.).

def: n. m. S. दुग्ध, Pālī. daddho ; Pr. duddh . 1. Milk. 1. Jaisā dudh vaisī budh . Prov. Your sense is as your mother's milk. 2. Hoṅtoṅ se abhī dūdh kī bū nahīṅ guī . Prov. His lips yet smack of his mother's milk. 3. Dūdh ko havvā le gayā . Wom. The goblin has flown away with the milk. 4. Dūdh phū, happā achchhā . Wom. Milk bitter, pap nice. ( Phū is opium applied to the nipples to keep off the child to be weaned from the breast). 5. Is kā dūdh phūlnā hai . Wom. Her milk is fattening. 6. Backchā rāt din meṅ ḍhāī ghaṛe dūdh pītā hai . Wom. A child drinks two and a half ghaṛās of milk (mother's milk) in the twenty-four hours. 7. Likkhe na paṛke, dūdh māreṅ kaṛhe! Prov. He can neither read nor write and he has plenty of milk to drink! 8. Dūdh meṅ kī makkhī kin ne chakkhī? Prov. Who would ever taste the fly in the milk? (Great cost and no advantage). 9. Dūdh kī sī makkhī nikāl-kar pheṅk dī . Prov. She cast me off as a fly from a bowl of milk. 10. Vahāṅ to chiṛyā kā dūdh bhī miltā hai . You can get even bird's milk there. 11. Jitnā ham ne dūdh pīyā hai, tumheṅ pānī bhī nahīṅ milegā , Prov. Saying. I have drank more milk than you will ever get water. 12. Dūdh bachā kar shādī kar do . Marry her away from her milk (blood-relations). 13. Gāë kā dūdh so māë kā dūdh . Prov. Cow's milk is as mother's milk. 2. ( Bot .) The juice or milk of plants; blastema. 3. Sect; caste. Āp kaun dūdh haiṅ? dūdh ubālnā, yā auṭānā, josh k. , v. a. To boil (milk). dūdhā bhātī , n. The ceremony of the bride and bridegroom eating a mess of boiled rice, milk, and sugar together, on the 4th day after marriage. dūdh utarnā , v. n. To flow (milk from the mother's breast). dūdh-ādhārī , n. m. One who lives on milk. dūdh baṛhānā, yā chhuṛānā , v. n. To wean (a child). dūdh-bhāī , n. m. A foster-brother ( birādar-i-rizāī ). dūdh bhar ānā , v. n. 1. To be full (udders, breasts). 2. To feel affection for. dūdh-bharī kaṭoriyāṅ , The breasts full of milk. dūdh-bahn , n. f. A foster sister. dūdh bechnā , v. n. 1. To sell milk. 2. To serve as a wet nurse. dūdh paṛnā , v. n. To form (the juice before the grain). dūdh yā dūdhī pilānā , v. a. To suckle; give suck to. dūdh-pilāī, dāī, dhāë , n. f. A wet nurse. dūdh pūt , n. m. Cattle and children. 1. Dūdh pūt qismāt se . Prov. Milk and children depend on fate. (Milk stands here for cattle or wealth). 2. Tū apne dūdh pūt ke āge pāë! Wom. A curse. dūdh pūt-vālī , n. f. A woman rich in cattle (wealth) and offspring. dūdh phaṭnā yā moṛ khānā , v. n. To turn (milk). dūdh-pītā yā god kā bachchā , n. m. A suckling; a child in arms. dūdh-pīte rupaë . Interest-bearing money; active capital. dūdh pīnā , v. n. To drink milk; to suck. Bachhṛe ne bāṅdh, gaū chūṅghe, Bachhṛā dūdh pīve saë, bahuaṛ, Tauṅh baiṭhī baiṭhī ūṅghe! Rus. Wom. Tie up the calf, it's drinking all the milk I say, My daughter-in-law! you just sit there and nod all day! dūdh jalnā , v. n. 1. To be burnt. 2 ( dūdh sūkhnā ) To be dry (a milch cow). dūdh churānā, yā chaṛhānā , v. n. To keep back its milk. Gāë dūdh chaṛhā gaī. dūdh dohnā, kāṛh'nā, yā nikālnā , v. a. To milk. dūdh dekhnā , v. a. To ascertain from the comparative thickness of the milk of a pregnant woman the sex of the unborn babe. Thick milk indicates a boy, thin milk a girl. 1. Yahāṅ baiṭhe kyā dūdh dete ho? Are you giving milk there? (Are you hatching eggs?). 2. Bak/?/ī ne dūdh dīyā, meṅgniyoṅ bharā . Prov. The milk the goat gave is full of its dung. (The benefit is made null by the conditions). dūdh sā , adj. White as milk; very white. Dūdh sī chāṅdī. dūdh kā pāṛā , n. A sucking calf or child. dūdh kā sā ubāl , n. m. A sudden fit of passion which is soon over. Dūdh kā sā ubāl āyā aur chalā gayā . Prov. saying. His rage boiled up like milk, and as suddenly went down. dūdh-kaṭṭū , E. n. m. A child not suckled by the mother for the full period owing to her pregnancy. Āge! larikvā dūdh-kaṭṭū bujhā hau, phir kuchh hau kā? Mag. Wom. I say! your child looks as though you had cut him off his milk. Are you in the family way again? dūdh ke dāṅt ; Sant. tovāḍāṭā , n. m. Milk-teeth. Abhi tumhāre dūdh ke dāṅt bhī nahīṅ ṭūṭe! Prov. You have not shed your milk-teeth yet! dūdh kī sī makkhī nikāl-kar pheṅknā , v. a. To cast off as a fly from milk. 1. Nikālā bazm se yūṅ apne ham ko tu ne jūṅ koī, Uṭhā-kar dūdh meṅ se, aë sitamgar! pheṅk de makkhī . Mārūf. O cruel tyrant! from your heart cast off as though You threw out of a bowl of milk a fly or so. 2. Ṭuṭbo na kalaī dudhvā ke daṅtvā, are birah ke kā jāne maramīyāṅ he nā? Bhoj. Song. Jantasārī. He has not shed his milk-teeth yet. What does he know of the pain of absence? dūdh kī tarah uphannā , v. n. To boil with rage (as milk boils over). dūdh-mā , n. f. Foster mother. dūdh-mūt yā gū mūt k. , Wom. v. n. To bring up an infant. dūdh-mogrī , n. Nerium coronarium ; the double white rose-bay, or oleander. Harris . dūdh-vālī , n. f. 1. A milkmaid. 2. A mother who suckles her child. dūdhoṅ nahāo, pūtoṅ phalo! A benediction. May you bathe in milk and rear numberless children! (May you abound in cattle and children!) dūdh-hāṅḍī, dudhaiṅḍī, duhāvnī , n. f. An earthen vessel for holding milk. āṭe kā dūdh , The starch of flour. chiṛyā kā dūdh, lit . Sparrow's milk. A mare's nest.
more: s. m. 1. Milk. 2. The juice (or milk) of certain bushes. دودهـ آدهاري dūdh-ādhārī ( दुग्धाहारी ) adj. Living on milk. دوده بڙهنا dūdh baṛhnā, v. n. To be weaned (a child). دودهـ يهاٴي dūdh-bhāʼī, s. m. A fosterbrother. دودهـ پلانا dūdh-pilānā, To suckle. دودهـ پلاٴي dūdh-pilāʼī, s. f. A wet nurse. دودهـ هنڐي dūdh-hanḍī, s. f. A vessel for holding milk, a milkpan.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi دودھ (dvdh /⁠dūdh⁠/) (c. 1411), / دود (dvd /⁠dūd⁠/) (c. 1635), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀤𑁆𑀥 (duddha), from Sanskrit दुग्ध (dugdhá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *dʰugdʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *dʰugʰ-tó-s, from *dʰewgʰ- (“to yield”).

def: s.m. A species of rice.
s. f. A ceremony performed the fourth day after marriage, wherein the bride and bridegroom eat milk, boiled rice, and sugar together.
adj. Giving milk, milch ( = dudhār).
more: Sant. (from tovā milk) adj. H. dūdh milk. A milk-giving animal. Dudhail gāë ki do lāteṅ bhī bhalī . Prov. Even two kicks may be borne from a milch cow.

def: adj. See ددهيل dudhail.
adj. = dudhal and dudhail, qq.v.
adj. See ددهار dudhār.
adj. Giving much milk; milk-giving, milch.
adj. Giving much milk; milk-giving, milch.
adj. Having milk, yielding milk, milky, milch; containing milky juice or sap (see dūdhiyā).
more: n. f. 1. The mother's breast. 2. Starch. 3. Plants with milky juice.
1. adj. Milky, containing milky juice. 2. s. f. Starch. 3. Name of several plants with milky juice; as, various species of Asclepias, Echites, Euphorbia, &c. Asclepias rosea, &c.

def: s.f. The breast (esp. of a mother or nursing woman); — starch; — name of several plants with milky juice (as various species of Asclepias, Echites, Euphorbia, &c.; see duddhī).

adj. Containing, or yielding, milk, &c. ( = dūdhī, q.v.); milk-white, white, pure; young, juicy, tender, green; — s.m. Name of several milky plants (see dūdhī and duddhī); — a young and tender head of maize or Indian corn, &c.
more: adj. 1. Giving milk; milch. 2. Milky; containing milky juice; 3. White; pure; like marble. 4. Raw; green; unripe. Talāo ke hare dudhiyā singhāṛe haiṅ! Delhi street cry. dūdh'iyā , n. Bhang mixed with milk. Āj to dūdhiyā chhane! Bhangaṛs. dudhiyā patthar, duddhī , n. A white stone used in building and in making various utensils.
1. adj. Milky. 2. s. m. See دودهي sign. 3. دودهيا پتهر dūdhiyā-patthar, A white stone of which plaster is made, and utensils are formed.
subs: s.m. A kind of white stone (used in building, and in making plaster, and utensils, &c.; see duddhī); — an opal.

def: s. A sweetmeat made of sugar, milk (or cream) and rice or wheat flour ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
milkman, dairyman
s.m. A kind of disease in the rice-plant.
v. a. (dakh.) To expel, drive away.

ety: mān = Pehl. mān = Zend nmāna, 'house,' &c., rt. man+ni = S. नि+मन्; cf. ḵẖān-o-mān, and māndan
def: s.m. An illustrious house, a noble family, a great tribe; house, family, race, generation.
more: s. m. Generation, race, noble family, house.

do-arthī, adj. & s.m. Double-meaning, ambiguous, equivocal; — double meaning, ambiguity; double-entendre

adj. lit. 'A two-days' guest'; temporary, transitory, fleeting, passing away (as beauty, &c.)
v.n. To die young, to die soon
s.m. A road dividing into two, or branching off in two directions; the junction of two roads

adj. & s.m. Double, two together; of two sorts; — two genuflexions in prayer; a prayer in which two genuflexions are made; — two intimate friends, an inseparable pair
(or ḍo-) gun, du-gunā, adj. (f. -ī), Double, two-fold, twice as much

(or do-) ganḍī ćiṭṭī, s.f. more: n. f. A shop ( hāṭ ). 1. Saudā bik gayā aur dukān rah gaī . Prov. The goods are sold, the shop remains. (Her bloom is gone, the frame remains). 2. Hot susaṅgat sahaj sukh, dukh kusaṅg ke ṭhān, Gandhī aur lūhār kī dekho baiṭh dukān . Brind satsahī. With good men you have ease, ill company's a root of pain, At the smithy's you get soot, at the perfumer's sweet scents your gain. dūkān baṛhānā yā baṅd k. , v. a. To close the shop. dūkān chalnā , v. n. To do a good business. dūkān-dār ; Pop. dukān-dār , n. m. A shopkeeper. dūkān-dārī ; Pop. dukān-dārī , n. f. Arts; trickery; gammon. dūkāṅ sī lagā'nā , v. a. 1. To set out in order as in a shop. 2. To scatter about as in a shop. dūkān k. , v. a. To keep…

def: n. m. S. दुःख afflict. Suffering ( pīṛā , 2); distress. 1. Ibn-i-Maryam huā kare koī, Mere dukh kī davā kare koī! Gālib. The Virgin Mary's son be he who may! Would some one only take my pain away! 2. Jāko Prabhū dārun dukh deṅhīṅ, Wākī mat pahle har leṅhīṅ . Rāmāyan. The man on whom God wills his heavy hand to lay, His guiding sense first from him he doth take away. 3. Jap, jog, virāg, mahā-makh, sādhan, dān, dayā, dam koṭī kare, Muni, sidh, suresh, Mahesh, Ganesh se sevat janm anek mare, Nigmāgam giyān Purān paṛhe, tapsa nal meṅ jug punj jare, Man se, pran rop kahe Tulsī, "ek Rām binā dukh kaun hare ?" Kabit. Though beads you tell, on His name dwell, give alms, your passions slay, Or mercy shew a million times, or sacrifice you pay, Or sage, or perfect man, Ganesh, Mahesh, you serve each day, Deep knowledge yours, versed in Purans, in fiery penance lay For ages, yet know that unblessed by Ram vain's your essay. dukh uṭhānā , v. n. 1. To bear pain; suffer; experience difficulties. 2. To take pains. dukh baṭānā , v. a. To share one's sorrows. Hameṅ kyā jo kisī kā dukh baṭāveṅ, Kisī ke vāste dunyā se jāveṅ? Jurat. What's it to me that I another's grief should share? Why for another's sake should I my life out wear? dukh bisārnā , v. n. To forget one's griefs. Kām usrā, dukh bisrā, chhāch na det ahīrā . Prov. His work all done, his grief forgot, no buttermilk from cowherd to be got. dukh bharnā, bhognā, yā bhugatnā , v. n. To labour; toil; suffer. Dukh bhareṅ bī Fā/?/khtah, aur kavve meve khāëṅ . Prov. The labour (of rearing) is my lady dove's, the fruit (brood) is devoured by the crow. (One soweth and another reapeth) dukh pānā , v. a. To suffer pain or trouble; to be afflicted. Dukh pāne-vāle laṛke jo dunyā meṅ āte haiṅ, Lachchhan sab unke pahle hī paichhāne jāte haiṅ . Nazīr. When children doomed to suffering in this world appear, Signs of their future from the first shown are quite clear. dukh-dāī , adj. Painful; grievous; troublesome; irksome; oppressive; vexatious. Sunahūṅ asantan ker subhāū! Bhūlehū saṅgatī kariye na kāū! Tin kar saṅg sadā dukh-dāī, Jimi kapilahīṅ ghāle harhāī . Rāmāyan. Now mark the ways of wicked men! Who in their company have been Have suffered grief and pain, as when Stray goats the house goat has let in. dukh dard kā sharīk , A partner in one's griefs and sorrows. dukh dard meṅ sharīk h. , v. n. To share one's griefs and sorrows; to sympathise or condole with one. dukh-dhandā , Labour; hard work; toil and trouble. dukh denā , v. a. To give pain ( taklīf denā ). dukh-sāgar , n. m. A sea of troubles ( bahr-i-gam); a world of misery. dukh sukh , Pain and pleasure. 1. Dukh sukh sab ke sāth lagā huā hai . Prov. Pain and pleasure are with us ever. 2. Dukh, sukh, nis din saṅg haiṅ, meṭ sake na koë, Jaise chhāyā deh kī nyārī nek na hoë . Dohā. Pain and pleasure with us ever, none can sever! As you wish it ne'er can be, no, parted never. dukh-kā-mārā , adj. Afflicted; wretched. dukh lagnā , v. n. To feel pain. Hoë na bishai birāg, bhavan basat bhayā chauthpan, Hirdai bahut dukh lāg, janm gayaū Harī-bhajt bin . Rāmāyan. The love of pleasure will not quit this frame, life's last stage's on, Sore is my heart, Har worship due unpaid, my life's thread spun. dukh-hartā, dukh bhanjan , n. One who relieves or removes pain or sorrow. dukh-haran , n. m. An anodyne.
more: s. m. 1. Pain, ail, ache, grief, affliction. 2. Labour, fatigue. 3. Annoyance, distress. دکهـ بهرنا dukh bharnā, To labour, to toil, to suffer distress or trouble. دکهـ پانا dukh pānā, To be afflicted. دکهـ داٴي dukh-dāʼī, or دکهـ داني dukh-dānī, adj. Giving or occasioning pain, troublesome, vexatious. دکهـ کا مارا dukh kā mārā, adj. Afflicted. دکهـ هرن दुःखहरण dukh-haraṇ, Anodyne. دکهـ هرتا दुःखहर्ता dukhahartā, Dispeller of grief or pain.

more: Wom. n. f. S. दुःखी A great sufferer; an unfortunate wretch. 1. Ban, bālak, aur bhaiṅs, ukhārī, Jeṭh mā/?/ ye chār dukhārī . Prov. Trees, babies, sugarcane, and buffalo, All sufferers are when hot May winds do blow. 2. Dukhiyā dukh rove, sukhiyā kamar ṭoë . Prov. The wretched weep and wail, the well-to-do their pockets feel. (Said of lawyers). 3. Kit bhag jāuṅ? kit uṭh jīūṅ? sūṅ maiṅ be-bas dukhyārī! Rus Wom. song. Ah whither shall I fly! where go! ah helpless wretched me! 4. Mohe nīkī lāge sūrat tihārī! Tum to ho Mohan apnī garaj ke! Maiṅ birhan dukhyārī! Song. Qadar. Such pleasure 'tis for me your face to see! You, Mohan, but pursue your pleasures free! Alas poor widowed heart! ah wretched me! 2. A widow ( rāṅd ). Mujh dukhyā ko na chheṛo. janam kā dukhiyā , A wretch foredoomed to life-long suffering.
adj. In distress or pain, unfortunate, poor, indigent, afflicted.

ety: Prk. दुक्खिअओ; S. दुःखित + कः; — dukhiyārā = dukhī+Prk. आलओ = S. आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Pained, afflicted, in pain or distress, distressed, suffering, ill, sick; affected, grieved, sorrowful, sad; unfortunate, poor, indigent, miserable, wretched; — afflicted one, sufferer, distressed person, unfortunate one, poor wretch.
more: adj. Afflicted, sorrowful, grieved, sad, wretched.

def: adj. Afflicted.
adj. दुःख + मनः Difficult; burdensome; tedious. Dūnī dukhman ho gaī .

ety: dukh˚ = Prk. दुक्ख(इ) or दुक्खे(इ) = S. दुःखय(ति), fr. दुःख
def: v.n. To pain, ache, smart, throb; to feel pity or compassion (for); to feel compunction or remorse.
more: v. n. 1. To ache; pain; throb. Abe, chhoṛ! merā pāoṅ dukhtā hai . Said by a thief. Let go my leg, fellow, it hurts. ☞ Native burglars, after they have made a hole through a wall, go in with their legs foremost, judging it better to lose a leg than the head. In this instance the legs being seized from within, the burglar, with a shrewd knowledge of human instincts, makes the prompt appeal upon which the other instinctively lets go. 2. To grudge; feel compunction or remorse; lie heavy on one's mind.
v. n. To ache, to pain, to smart.
wety: Urdu verb set دِکْھنا (dikhnā) دیکْھنا (dekhnā) دِکھانا (dikhānā) دِکھوانا (dikhvānā)Intransitive form of دیکھنا .

def: s.m. Great, or constant, suffering; distress, calamity, misfortune, woe; hard labour, toil
subs: v.n. To be afflicted, or distressed, &c., to be overtaken by misfortune
v.n. To live a life of toil and trouble
(apnā), To bewail (one's) woes or troubles, to tell (one's) misery, &c.
(apnā), To go about telling (one's) grievances or troubles.

def: Wom. n. m. 1. Great or constant sufferings; hard labour; a life of suffering. 1. Morī toṛ daī kāyā sārī, Pīsan kā dukhṛā bhārī . Wom. Rus. song. Hard toil it is to turn and turn the mill, My bones are broken as I grind on still. 2. Chār paise kā dukhṛā bhī nahīṅ ātā . Wom. Not even a two-penny hard job can I get. 3. Na pīse na kūṭe, na dukhṛā kare, /?/Khudā aisī ٴaurat ko gārat kare! She 'll neither grind nor pound, nor sweat for daily bread, Out on such creatures? would to God that she were dead! 4. Ham apnā dukhṛā meṅ lagal hiyā, tohrā saṅgh nijārā māre ke jāë? Mag. I am in trouble here, and you want me to flirt with you! 2. The story of one's sufferings; a tale of woe; a sad story. 1. Ghar ghar sās kā dukhṛā rotī phirti hai . Wom. From house to house she repeats the story of her sufferings at the hands of her mother-in-law. 2. Na more dīyā bātī, na more jhupṛā, Phūṅkūṅ phūs, dikhāūṅgī mukhṛā: Āj rah jā, bālam! kahūṅ dukhṛā! Rus. song. Nor lamp nor light, nor e'er a hut I own, Some straw picked up I'll fire; my face just shown. To-night, dear, stay! but hear my sad heart's moan! dukhṛā paṛnā , Wom. v. n. 1. To be afflicted. 2. To become a widow ( rāṅḍ ho jānā ). dukhṛā pīṭnā , v. n. To work or labor hard; to have a hard life of it. Din bhar dukhṛā pīṭtī hai . Wom. dukhṛā ronā , Wom. v. n. To tell the story of one's sufferings; narrate one's sorrows.

more: n. f. S. दुहितृ A daughter ( beṭī ); a girl ( laṛkī ). dukhtar-i-rabība ; H. sautelī beṭī , n. f. A stepdaughter. dukhtar-i-raz , Poet. n. f. Daughter of the grape; wine. Aë Zauq! dekh! dukhtar-i-raz ko na mūṅh lagā! Chhuṭṭī nahīṅ hai mūṅh se yeh kāfir lagī huī! Zauq. O Zauq! let not the daughter of the grape thy lips touch e'er! This godless wretch will never leave if once she sticks on there.

wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀳 (dullaha), ultimately from Sanskrit दुर्लभक (durlabhaka, literally "hard to attain or gain"), from दुर्- (dur-, “hardly”) + लभक (labhaka, “attained”), itself a compound of the root लभ् (labh, “to gain, attain”) + -क (-ka, adjectival suffix from Proto-Indo-Iranian *-kas).

def: s.f. = dhulenḍī, q.v. s.f. The first day of the month Ćait (on which it is the practice of the Hindūs to scatter ashes); the second day of the Holī festival (on which they throw a red powder called abīr or gulāl on one another).

n. f. S. धूलि dust, उड्डी to fly. The first day of the month Chait , or the second day of the Holī , on which it is the practice to throw ashes, abīr, gulāl , etc. on one another. The ashes thrown are typical of the dust which begins to be blown at this time.

ety: Prk. दुल्लहिणी and दुल्लहिआ; S. दुर्लभ+इनी and इका
def: s.f. A bride (see dulhā).
n. f. 1. A bride. 2. A wife.

n. f. 1. A bride. Jogī the so ram gaë, pherī de gaë gāoṅ, Buṛhyā kar-ke ṭeran lāge, dulhan miṭ gayo nāoṅ . Doha. The village roamed, the mendicant's now lost to sight, The old dame now she's called who erst was bride behight. 2. A married woman; a feme covert . 1. Gaune kī rāt ko bhor, hī kone meṅ baiṭh rahī dulhī an-bole, Hāth se chhālī chhipāë-ke sundar nār navāe durāë kapole, Dekhan ko juṛ āīṅ, sab tīyī, nanad, jiṭhānī kareṅ yoṅ kalole, Ek haṅse ek bāṅh gahe, ek aṅchrā eṅch-ke ghūṅgaṭ khole . Sundar Singār. Next morn the bride apart and silent in a corner sat, Her bosom with her hands concealed, head bent, cheeks hid she sat, The women 'bout her press, the sisters-in-law with jest her hail, One laughs, one holds her by the arm, one draws aside her veil. 2. A wife ( jorū ). 1. Falāne kī dulhan . 2. Bahut rahī bābal ghar, dulhin! chal tohe pīyā ne bulāī, Kar le singhār, chatar albetī, sājan ke ghar jānā hogā ! Bhajan. Mauj. Your father's house hath seen you long, your husband calls you now, Adorn yourself, bright sprightly one! t'your husband's house now go.

ety: Pehl. dum; Zend duma, rt. du = S. धू
def: s.f. Tail; end, extremity; — a constant follower (see next)
more: n. f. H. pūṅch , 1. Tail. 2. End; extremity. 1. Hāthī nikal gayā, dum rah gaī . Prov. The elephant's got out, his tail remains. (Said when very little remains to do of a great work). 2. Chhoṭe se Gāzī mīyāṅ, baṛī sī dum! Prov. A warrior short, with such a length of tail! 3. Khaṛā to rah, terī dum meṅ rassī bāṅdhūṅ . Just stand still for a moment and I'll tie a string to your tail. (Said to frighten children away). 4. Pichhle misre kī baṛh gaī hai dum . Inshā. The last verse has too long a tail. 3. A follower; an imitator. dum-dār , adj. Having a tail. dum-dār tārā ; H. pūṅchhal tārā ; Wom. jhāṛū ; A. zū-zanab , n. m. ( Ast .) A comet. dum dabā-kar, yā nok dum bhāgnā , v. n. 1. To run away with one's tail under his legs. 2. To turn tail; show the white feather; slink away; wriggle out of. Phr.—To drop one's tail; hang down one's crest; take to one's heels; save one's bacon; cut and run; f…

s. f. Tail, end, extremity. دم دار dum-dār, Tailed, having a tail. دم دبانا dum dabānā, To run away, to put the tail between the legs. دم دبا جانا dum dabā jānā, To run away. دم علم کرنا dum aﻌlam karnā, To run away. دم لابه dum-lāba, s. The wagging of the tail (as, of a cat or dog when pleased). دم لابه کرنا dum-lāba karnā, To wag the tail (as, a dog or cat when pleased). دم مين گهسنا dum meṅ ghusnā, To take protection.
subs: s.m. Tail of a paper kite; a tail, generally; (met.) one who is ever at the heels of another (as a child that follows its mother about), a constant follower
adv. With the tail between the legs
v.n. To put the tail between the legs, to drop the tail, to turn tail, to run away ( = dum dabā-kar bhāgnā)
adj. In good condition, plump, fat
v.n. 'To make the tail erect'; to run away with the tail stuck out (as an elephant, &c.)
s.m. The root, or place of growth, of the tail (of a bird, or other animal), the coccyx
s.m. The wagging of the tail (as of a dog, when pleased)
To wag the tail ( = dum hilānā)
(-kī), To creep under the tail (of, as pups under a bitch); to take refuge or protection (with); to dangle or run (after), to follow about, to cling (to, as a child to its mother's skirt)
To shake or wag the tail (as a dog, &c.).

(or ḍo-) gun, du-gunā, adj. (f. -ī), Double, two-fold, twice as much

(or do-) ganḍī ćiṭṭī, s.f. A go-between, a fomenter of quarrels, one who flatters both parties in a dispute (the expression is taken from a game in which ćiṭṭī or tamarind seeds are used as dice, the seed being marked or rubbed on one face or side; and if it be fraudulently marked on both sides, it is called du-ganḍī)

(-ko), To strike (one) with both hands at one time

adj. In good condition, plump, fat

To wag the tail ( = dum hilānā)

To shake or wag the tail (as a dog, &c.).

more: s. m. 1. A tail. 2. The outer angle or corner of the eye. 3. adv. After, behind. دنباله دار dumbāla-dār, adj. Tailed. ستاره دنباله دار sitāra-e-dumbāla-dār, A comet.

adj. (in comp. = dūnā), Double, &c.; see dūnā

def: A tract of country at the foot of hills, a valley.
adj. Low, base, vile, ignoble, grovelling, mean, paltry, poor, inferior, contemptible
adj. Base; ignoble (H. pājī ); poor. dūṅ-himmat , adj. Mean-spirited; low-minded.
more: n. f. S. दात्र fire. An excess of heat or thirst. dauṅ lagnā , v. n. lit . To be excessively thirsty. 1. To burn with love; pine in absence. Hirde aṅdar dauṅ lagī, dhuāṅ na parghat hoë, Jā tan lāge so lakhe, dūjā lakhe na koë . The flame that's in the heart no smoke at all doth show, He only sees who feels, no other it can know. 2. To long for; crave. Tum ko to iskī dauṅ lag rahī hai .

def: adj. (f. -ī), Two-fold, double, twice as much, twice, multiplied by two ( = dugnā)
more: adj. S. द्विगुण, Pr. dūunā . See دگنا dūnā dū'nā, dugnā dugnā , adv. More and more; all the more; Jūṅ jūṅ manā karo, dūnā dūnā hotā hai . The more you check him the worse he is. dūn kī lenā , v. n. lit . to make oneself out twice as great, etc. To boast; brag. Poṅchhte phirte the kal tak nāk tum, Āj mujh se dūn kī lene lage! Wiping your nose you went about but yesterday, Today your boastful airs before me you display!
n. m. S. दमन, Southern wood; Artemisia lactifolia .

n. m. Leaves folded up in the shape of a cup for holding flowers, sweetmeat, etc. khaṛā daunā denā , v. a. To offer a daunā full of sweetmeats, etc. in fulfilment of a vow; to pay one's vows. Jald bāzār se tū lā daunā, Ki dadā! dāṅgī maiṅ khaṛā daunā . Wom. Raṅgīn. Quick from the market now for me a dauna bring, O nurse! I needs must make my votive offering.
s. m. The name of a flower. (A species of Artemisia).
s. m. Leaves folded up in the shape of a cup for holding betel, flowers, sweetmeats, &c.
subs: dūnā-dūnā, adj. & adv. Twice as much again; more and more, all the more; — s.m. The double of a thing added thereto.
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa दूण (dūṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Prakrit 𑀤𑀼𑀉𑀡 (duuṇa), 𑀤𑀺𑀉𑀡 (diuṇa), from Sanskrit द्विगुण (dviguṇa).

def: adj. contrac. of dūnā, q.v.
s.m. A large kettle-drum ( = dundubhī); — noise, din, tumult, uproar, riot; outrage, injustice ( = andher)
more: n. m. S. दुन्दु a sort of drum. 1. A kind of kettle-drum. 2. Noise; yell; scream. Aisā dund mach rahā hai ki koī kisī kī nahīṅ suntā . 3. Riot; tumult. 4. Injustice ( andher ). dund machānā , v. a. 1. To make a noise or riot. 2. To oppress ( andher k. , 1.). Kachahrīyoṅ meṅ ek dund machā huā hai .
s. A large kettledrum. See دندبهي dundubhī.
subs: To make a noise, create an uproar; to perpetrate an outrage, to practise oppression.

s.m. (dialec.), A dust-cloud, a whirlwind.

more: Panj. n. m. A whirlwind. 1. Āṅdhī chālī, dūṅḍe uṭṭhe, ib mat āg jalā, kāman! Rus. song. 2. Sāsaṛ kā bolā bhūṅḍā rī jīv basole ḍhālāṅ chhole, Gam kā ṭhāve ḍūṅḍā rī . Wom. Rus. song. The mother-in-law's words are awful to bear, As chips from a chisel, or grief's whirlwind to scare!

s.m. = ḍunḍubh, q.v. s.m. (f. -ā), A kind of lizard without feet, Amphisbæna; a kind of snake.

def: s.m. A sort of red worm.
adj. (f. -ī), Deep, profound.
adj. Deep. Bachhṛā bhī tatte nāhīṅ thamtā! Ḍūb kue bhītar ḍūṅghe! Rus. Said by the mother-in-law. The calf even you cant hold in. Would you were drowned in some deep well!
more: n. m. 1. A trough. 2. A canoe; boat. 3. A ladle made of the shell of a cocoanut with a long wooden handle. 4. A hot-water or beefsteak dish; a tureen. 5. An entrèe . ḍoṅgāī, ḍūṅgāī, ḍoṅgān , n. f. Depth. ḍoṅgī, dauṅgī, ḍeṅgī , n. f. A small boat.

n. m. Dim. ḍūṅgrī , n. f. 1. A hill; high ground; mount. Daham lage, aur māchhar kāṭeṅ, āṅkh jhapakne pāve nā. Ḍūṅgar ḍhāl kaṭaiṅ saiṅ ratyāṅ, tauṅh auṅṭhī silgāve nā! Rus. To the mother-in-law. It is so hot, mosquitoes bite, sleep hath my eyes forswore, The night sits heavy as a hill, Oh! dont inflame me more! 2. A hilly country. Ḍūṅgar jaṅgal līye binā ke tanne, balam, sare nāhīṅ, Ḍūṅgar par ghās uge nāhīṅ . W. Rus. song. Will nothing serve you, husband, but hill jungle you must take on lease? Nograss upon the hill tops grows.

more: n. m. Dim. of dānā , q. v. Dānā dunkā .

ety: fem. of ادني adnā, 'nearer,' &c.; rt. دنو 'to be or become near'
def: s.f. (lit. 'Nearer, nearest, former'); the present world, the present life or state of existence; the people of this world, people; a whole world, a multitude; — worldly enjoyments or blessings, worldly goods, the good things of this life, wealth, riches
more: n. f. دنو low. 1. The world and its inhabitants; a multitude; the goods of this world. 1. Dunyā ba-ummed qāyam hai , Prov. The world is sustained by hope. 2. Dunyā khāiye makkar se, roṭī khāīye shakkar se . Prov. The world just cheat, your bread with sugar eat. 3. Dīn se dunyā rakhnī mushkil hai . Prov. To win the world is a harder task than winning Heaven. 2. Wealth; riches. Kaho kitnī dunyā chhoṛī? dunyā-dār , adj. Wordly; secular. duny-dār , n. m. One who is absorbed in worldly affairs. dunyā-dārī , n. f. 1. Secular or worldly affairs. 2. Worldly goods; wife and children. 3. Worldliness. 4. Carnal intercourse. 5. A show of politeness.

(either as being near and present or as worthless, from دنو) s. f. 1. The world. 2. ( met. ) People, goods of the world. دنيا پواتاني پڙنا dunyā po utāne paṛnā (dakh.) To be proud or haughty. دنيا دار dunyā-dār, Worldly, a person of the world. دنيا داري dunyā-dār-ī, s. f. Economy, worldliness, attention to the concerns of the world. دنيا طلبي dunyā-t̤alab-ī, s. f. Seeking the goods of the world, worldliness.
subs: adj. & s.m. Worldly; worldly-minded; — a person of the world; one absorbed in worldly affairs, a mammonist, one possessed of the good things of this world, a rich man
s.f. Worldliness, attention to the concerns of the world; worldly affairs; economy; mammon, worldly goods or possessions, riches; wife and children, family; — a show of politeness
s.f. Seeking the goods of the world, worldliness
The world and whatever is in it.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دُنْیَا (dunyā), borrowed from Arabic دُنْيَا (dunyā), from أَدْنَى (ʔadnā), from دَنِيّ (daniyy). First attested in c. 1435 as Middle Hindi دنیا (dnya).

oblique plural of دُنْیا (dunyā, “world”)

dupatta, scarf

def: n. m. lit . having two breadths. A cloth thrown loosely over the shoulders; a wrapper. du-paṭṭā tān ke sonā , v. n. 1. To sleep with the sheet drawn over one's head. 2. To sleep with a mind at ease or a light heart. 3. To sleep the sleep of death. du-paṭṭā yā chādar hilānā, yā phirānā , v. a. To hold out a flag of truce; to offer to surrender. du-paṛtā , adj. Of two-folds; double. du pushtah k. , v. a. To print on both sides. du-palṛī , n. f. A native cap consisting of two pieces. du-pal'kā , n. m. lit . having two eyelids. 1. A kind of pigeon. 2. A kind of stone for a ring. do-phār , ( Bot .) Bipartite. do-pahar; Rus. do-pahrī , n. f. ट्ठिप्रहर Midday; noon. Jo yoh līlā du-pahre gāve, Chhaitīs bhojan rasoī meṅ pāve . Bhajan. Who Krishna's praises sings two hours each day, Him six and thirty dishes will repay. do pahar par ānā . ( Ast .) Transit. do pahar pahle , adv. Ante meridiem ; A. M. do pahar chakkar, garmī manḍal , ( Ast .) Meridional lines. do-pahar ḍhal'nā , v. n. To pass the meridian. Ab woh husn nahīṅ rahā, do pahar ḍhal gaī . Your beauty all for aye hath flown, Your noonday waneth to sundown. do pahar ḍhale; Post meridiem ; P. M. do-pahar kī uṅchāī . ( Ast .) Meridian altitude. du-paharyā , adj. Noontide; meridional. du-paharyā , n. m. 1. ( gul-i-dupaharyā ) A flower of a deep carmine color; Pentapetes phœnicea . 2. A child born at noon. 3. A naughty child. du-piyāzā , n. m. A rich meat curry in which a double quantity of onions is used. du-tārā , n. m. A two-stringed instrument. du-taī , n. f. A piece of cloth having two breadths, used for lying upon. do ṭūk javāb denā , v. a. To give a short and decisive answer; settle a matter with a word; to cut the matter short; give a flat denial. do-ṭūk, yā do k. , v. n. 1. To cut in two. 2. To separate; disjoin. do-ṭūk h. , v. n. 1. To be separated, divided, at feud. 2. To be adjusted, settled. do-jīyā ; Sant. bar jīvī , adj. Pregnant; with child. do-jībhā, du-jibhā , adj. Pālī, dvijivho . Double-tongued. do-jī se, yā du-jīyā h. , v. n. To be pregnant. du-chār h , v. n. To meet; have an interview ( āṅkheṅ chār h .). Shab ko diloṅ meṅ wāh! wāh! zor mazoṅ ke tār the! Ham se do chār yār thā, yār se ham do chār the! Nazīr. Last night such streams of pleasure coursed my veins within! Her eyes in mine transfixed, my eyes in hers locked in! du-chāh , The second reservoir to which water is raised for the purpose of irrigation. du-chittā, du-dilā , adj. 1. Of two minds; doubtful; wavering. 2. Abstracted; absent. du-chittā, do-dilā , n. m. Suspense ( dubdhā , 1); absence of mind. du-chand , Poet. du-bālā , adj. Double; two-fold; as much again. du-chand; sazā . ( Law ) Double punishment. du-chobā , adj. A double-poled tent. du-chhattā , A house with a double roof; a thatched roof sloping both ways. du-harfī , (P. maë ), n. f. Wine. du-dastah , adj. Double-handed; having two handles. du-dastah khilāl , ( Cards ). One man winning a slam or rubber from two other competitors. do din kā mehmān , adj. lit . a guest for two days. Transitory; transient; passing away. Husn yeh do din kā hai mehmān, haṅs le, bol le! Nazīr. Beauty's at best a two days' guest, laugh and enjoy the merry jest. do din na pakaṛnā , v. n. To die all too soon. do do dāne ko phirnā , v. n. To beg from door to door for a crust of bread. du-dhārā, du-dhārī , adj. Two-edged (sword). du-rāhā , n. m. A road branching off in two directions; the fork of a road. du-rukhā , adj. See du-bhesyā . 1. The same on both sides; not having a wrong side. 2. Having two colors or aspects. do-ras , n. m. Clay and sandy soil. du-rasā , n. m. A mixture of two kinds of tobacco. du-raggā, doglā , n. m. A mongrel; half-bred. du-raṅgā , adj. 1. Of two colors; piebald. 2. Hypocritical. du-raṅgī, du-ruī , n. f. Duplicity; hypocrisy; double-dealing. Du-raṅgī chhoṛ de, ek raṅg ho jā! Sarāsar mom ho, yā saṅg ho jā! Eschew hypocrisy, one color be! Or be a stone, or soft wax be! du-rūyā , adj. Double-faced. du-rūyā , adv. On both sides. du-rūyā sikhar kāṭ , ( Math .) Hyperbola. du-zānū baiṭhnā , v. n. To sit on one's hams or haunches; to kneel. du-sār honā , v. n. To penetrate; pierce. du-sālā , adj. Biennial. du-sālā , n. m. Lands that have been two years under cultivation. dusrāṅd' , n. f. An inmate of the house; a chum; a fellow-lodger. du-sūtī , n. m. A kind of cloth, the threads of which are double. du-serī , n. f. A weight of two sers. du-shākhā , n. m. 1. The fork of a tree. 2. A pair of legs. 3. Vulva fœminœ. du-shāl'ā; Rus. dusālā , n. m. S. शाढ cloth. A pair of shawls. 1. Kuchh māl dushālā nahīṅ kambal ke agāṛī; Yeh mol meṅ bhārī hai to voh tol meṅ bhārī . Āzād. Devotees. No shawl can with a blanket vie, Less weighty though its price be high. 2. Kamar ṭūṭe ranḍī ki, bhaṛve oṛheṅ dushālā . Prov. The harlot breaks her back, the pimp fine shawls disports. 3. Koi oṛhe sāl dusālā, morā pīavā oṛhe kārī kāmaryā . Wom. Some wear rich shawls, my love a blanket black. dushāle-posh , n. m. One who sports a shawl; hence, a swell. dushāle meṅ lapeṭ-ke lagānā , v. a. To administer sugared poison; to cover one's gall with a coating of honey; cover the point of one's dagger with velvet. du-tarfah, du-tarfī , adj. Having two sides; two-sided; mutual. du-amlī , n. f. The rule of two masters. du-faslī, du-sākhī, du-har, du-phalī, du-tanābī , (opp. of ek-fardah or rice lands). n. f. Land yielding two crops in a year. do karnā , v. a. To divide into two parts; to bisect; cut in two. do kauṛī kī bāt kar denā , v. a. To disgrace oneself; lay one's honor in the dust. du-gānā , n. m. 1. Two prostrations at prayer. 2. Two intimate female friends. 3. A woman who engages with another woman in sexual intercourse with the aid of a dildo. du-ganḍī chiṭṭī , n. A go-between; one who flatters both parties in a dispute. The idiom is taken from a gambling game played with tamarind seeds used as dice. The seed rubbed and marked for this purpose is called chittī , and if it be fraudulently marked in the same way on both sides it is called du-ganḍī. do-ghaṛ , Two vessels full of water placed one over the other. It is a good omen to pass the do-ghaṛ on one's right, and it is always so placed for a wedding party. du-lāvah , n. m. A well with two lāos , or well buckets and ropes. du-lattī , n. f. A kick with the two hind legs of a quadruped. du-lattī mārnā, yā pheṅknā , v. a. To kick out with the two hind legs (esp. a horse). do-laṛā, dolṛā , n. m. 1. A necklace of two strings. 2. A piece of cloth consisting of two breadths. du-laṛā , adj. Of two rows or strings. du-lohī , n. f. A scimitar made of two plates of steel joined together. du-mahlā, du-manzila, du-khanā , adj. Of two floors or stories (a house, etc.); of two decks (a ship). du-mānī, zū-mānī ; H. du-arthī , n. f. A double meaning; a double-enṭendre. du-mūṅhā , E; du-mūī , W. n. lit . having two mouths. A serpent with two mouths. do muṅh haṅs lenā , v. n. To laugh and amuse oneself in moderation, or within conventional limits. Chār meṅ baiṭh-ke haṅs letī hūṅ do muṅh maiṅ bhī , Wom. Rangīn, Jī meṅ khush hotī hūṅ har dam kī mulāqāt se kam . I like to laugh and while away an hour with three or four, The pleasure of the heart is less from meeting, every hour. du-nātī , adj. Double-barrelled (a gun). du-nokī , ( Bot .) Bifid. du-varqī , n. f. lit . a book with two leaves. 1. A small book. 2. ( Fac .) Pudendum muliebre. du-varqī kā sabaq paṛhnā , Slang. v. n. To engage in carnal intercourse. du-hattaṛ , n. m. Beating with both hands. du-hattaṛ mārnā , v. n. To beat with both hands at one time. du-hattī , adj. With both hands. band o bast-i-du-sālā . Biennial settlement.
s.m. corr. of du-paṭṭā, q.v.s.v. do, 'two.' s.m. For comp. having du or do for the first member, see s.v. do, 'two.'
intj. Away! begone! avaunt! — also, contract. of dūr, 'far,' q.v. (used in comp., e.g. dur-bāsī; dur-darśan-yantra)
more: Mah. Wom. intj. Stand off! See alag , intj. dur dur k. , Wom. v. a. To drive or turn out. Dur dur karūṅ, tau dauṛā āve, Khan āṅgan, khan lāhar jāve, Dehal chhoṛ kahiṅ nahīṅ sūtā. 'Kyoṅ sakhī, sājan?' 'Nā sakhī, kūtā .' Riddle Mukarnī. 'Out! out!' I cry, but still to me he 'll running come, Sometimes within, sometimes without he roves playsome, He never leaves the threshold, never sleeps elsewhere. 'A lover, dear?' 'No, dear, the dog who's present e'er.' dur dur phiṭ phiṭ honā , v. n. To be an object of scorn. dur-muë, dur-muī , Mah. Wom. intj. Be off! go away! ' Chiṛyā, chiṛyā, kivāṛ kholyo.' Us ne kahā, 'dur muë! merī āṅkheṅ dukkheṅ .' Children's story.

A prefix. Opp. of su good. dur-āchār , adj. Misconduct ( bad chalnī ). dur-āchārī , n. m. A bad man. dur-bachan , n. m. Bad words; abuse. dur-ba', dublā , adj. Pālī, dubba'o . Difficult of access; inaccessible; unattainable. 2. Deep; profound. dur-gandh , adj. Pālī, duggandhī . Bad smell. dur-labh , adj. Pālī, dullabha . Hard to obtain; difficult to find. dur-matī , adj. Pālī, dummatī . 1. Evil-minded. 2. Foolish.

ety: P. dūr, fr. Zend dūra = S. dūra
def: adj. Distant, remote, far (from, -se), at a distance, a long way off; — s.f. Distance, remoteness ( = dūrī); — adv. Far, afar, far away, to a distance, beyond (with abl.); — intj. = dūr ho, q.v.
more: adj. S. दूर, Pālī, dūro; Z. durā . Far; distant; remote. 1. Aqlmandoṅ kī dūr balā . Prov. Calamity keeps far from the sensible. 2. Apnā ghar dūr se sūjhtā hai . Prov. One sees his own house afar off. (We have all sharp eyes for our own interests). 3. Jinhoṅ ke kān nahīṅ, dūr kī woh sunte haiṅ, Jo kān-vāle haiṅ baiṭhe woh sir ko dhunte haiṅ . The times out of joint. Nazir. Who have no ears hear e'er so far, Who ears possess distracted are. 4. Dūr ke ḍhol suhāvne . Prov. Distant music is sweet. (Distance lends enchantment to the view). 5. Pās kā kuttā, na dūr kā bhāī . Prov. A dog at hand is better than a brother far off. 6. Sab se behtar hai, miyān, sāhab salāmat dūr kī . Prov. Distant acquaintance is best. (Familiarity breeds contempt). 7. Zamīṅ sakht aur āsmān dūr hai . Prov. The earth's too hard, the sky so far! (Ah! whither can I fly!) 8. Āṅkhoṅ se dūr, dil se dūr . Prov. Out of sight…

1. s. f. Distance. 2. adj. At a distance, far, distant, remote. In Sans. the reverse of उप . دورانديش dūr-andesh, adj. Provident, foreseeing, penetrating, circumspect, sagacious, wise, prudent. دورانديشي dūr-andesh-ī, s. f. Foresight, forecast, circumspection, sagacity, wisdom, carefulness. دورباد dūr bād, God forbid. دوربهاگنا dūr bhāgnā, To abstain from, to abhor, abominate, avoid, shun. دوربين dūr-bīn, 1. s. m. A spy-glass, a telescope. 2. adj. Having the quality of shewing a distant object. 3. Far-seeing; ( met. ) prudent, provident, ingenious. دوربيني dūr-bīn-ī, s. f. Foresight, prudence. عقل دوربين aﻌḳl-i-dūr-bīn, Solid judgment. دورپار dūr pār, God forbid (a female phrase). دوردراز dūr darāz, adj. Far, very distant, long. دوردست dūr-dast, adj. Difficult to reach, remote, inaccessible. دورکرنا dūr karnā, To remove, to dispel, to reject, to avert, to keep at a distance. دورهونا dūr …

(Zend dūraēpāra; S. dūra-pāra), intj. 'May it be far away' (from me, or from you, &c.), far be it! God forbid! (a phrase used commonly by women)
v.t. To send away to a distance, to drive away
s.m. Far-sightedness; long-sightedness; foresight; discernment, prudence ( = dūr-bīnī and dūr-andeshī)
vulg. dūr-darsī, adj. & s.m. Far-seeing, long-sighted; foreseeing, provident, prudent, discerning, &c. ( = dūr-andesh); — a vulture; — a prophet, seer, sage; a learned man, a teacher
adj. Distant, out of reach, difficult to reach, remote, inaccessible
s.f. The being out of reach, or difficult to reach, remoteness, inaccessibleness
(-se), To remain far away or apart (from); to abstain (from), &c. ( = dūr bhāgnā)
adj. Situated at a distance, standing afar off, far off, remote
adj. (f. -ī), Distant, far removed (as a relation); deep, profound (as thought); far-fetched, out-of-the-way; long and sharp, or thorough good (as a scolding, &c.)

v.t. To put far away, keep at a distance; to remove (from, -se); to avert; to dispel; to put away or aside; to reject; to get rid of, to part with; to dismiss; to eject, dispossess; to do away with, abolish; to put out of sight, conceal; to efface
adj. & s.m. Going far, far-reaching; — one who goes to a distance
vulg. dūr-bāsī, adj. & s.m. Residing in a distant land, or in a foreign country, outlandish; — one who resides at a great distance, &c.; an outlandish person
adj. & s.m. Being, or situated, at a distance, far-removed, remote; residing at a distance; — one who is at a distance

intj. Begone! be off! away! avaunt! — dūr honā, v.n. To be distant, be far off, to be or stand remote, to be far beyond (with abl.); to be removed, be averted, be dispelled; to be effaced; to be off, get out of the way, make (oneself) scarce, disappear, decamp.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀤𑀽𑀭 (dūra), from Sanskrit दूर (dūrá). Also influenced by Classical Persian دور (dūr).

part. adj. Removed to a distance, placed afar off.

def: adj. & s.m. Difficult; bad, sinful, wicked; concealed; — bad course, evil ways, evil, sin.
v. a. See ڐريانا ḍuriyānā.

ety: ḍorī, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To lead about with a rope or string (as a horse, &c.), to lead in hand
more: (from ڐوري) v. a. To lead about (as, a horse), to lead in hand.

def: s.m.f. A small receptacle or casket for gems, &c.
more: s. m. f. A little casket in which they deposit gems.

ety: A. durj+P. dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.m. A small casket, a cabinet, box (for jewels).
more: s. A small casket, a cabinet, box (for jewels).
